attributes.content:
## Overview

The Artboard card gives the workspace a structured home for free-form canvas for arranging cards, images, and annotations spatially. Visual thinking space.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Artboard usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Artboard can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Note]]** -- Note can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Artboard.
- **[[Article]]** -- Article can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Artboard.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Artboard into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Artboard can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Artboard can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Artboard as the structured home for free-form canvas for arranging cards, images, and annotations spatially. Visual thinking space and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Artboard when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Artboard stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
